content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reject fr-5-f1c7b7b6 because it treats an intentional safety limit as a defect: existing rows with `plan_hash=None` cannot be safely backfilled from the mutable snapshot without attempt-scoped provenance, and they are still repairable when a later replay provides an explicit `plan_hash`. Accept fr-5-39bc594e because the fs adapter still duplicates the journal payload schema and the added tests are example-based, so optional-field drift can still escape compile/test-time detection.

## Votes

- `fr-5-f1c7b7b6`: Reject
  The code intentionally refuses snapshot-based backfill for pre-existing rows because `snapshot.plan_hash` is mutable and may have advanced after the attempt started; with a legacy `TaskRunEntry { plan_hash: None }`, the store has no attempt-scoped evidence that the current snapshot hash is the right one. That makes auto-repair from the snapshot unsafe, not merely incomplete. The amendment also overstates the impact: such rows are not permanently unrepairable, because both the running-row and terminal-row repair paths will fill `plan_hash` when a later replay supplies an explicit value. Leaving the field unset when provenance is unknown is the safer behavior, and the service regression tests explicitly assert that legacy rows must not be mislabeled after plan evolution.
- `fr-5-39bc594e`: Accept
  `src/adapters/fs.rs` still defines its own `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` structs instead of sharing a single canonical payload type with `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs`, so there is no structural coupling between producer and parser. The new tests only round-trip one concrete payload shape; they do not guarantee failure when a future field is optional or conditionally omitted from serialization. In that case the duplicated fs structs can remain stale while the tests still pass, so the amendment correctly identifies an unresolved drift-detection gap.
